hero.subtitle: Import your GPX trace from Strava, customise your poster with dozens of styles, and download your high-quality PDF instantly. Marathon, trail, ultra — every race deserves to be framed.
hero.previewLabel: Preview of some RunMemories creations — infinitely customisable

problem.label: The problem
problem.headline: Honestly — is this really how you want to remember your races?
problem.subtitle: You trained for months. You suffered. You finished. That moment deserves to be celebrated — not buried in your phone.
problem.microcopy: Free preview, no credit card required

pains.title: Race photos pile up on your phone
pains.desc: Hundreds of blurry shots, never looked at, never shared. Your 4-hour marathon deserves better than a finish-line selfie.

pains.title: The medal collects dust in a drawer
pains.desc: You put it away after the race. It doesn't tell the story — the 3 months of training, the pain at km 38, the pride at the finish.

pains.title: You want to share your achievement, but Strava screenshots look terrible
pains.desc: An app screenshot doesn't do justice to what you accomplished. It's not what you want to see every morning on your wall.

howItWorks.label: How it works
howItWorks.headline: Your poster in
howItWorks.subtitle: Get a race poster with zero technical skills. The editor does all the work.

steps.title: Import your activity
steps.desc: Connect Strava in 1 click or import a GPX/KML file from Garmin, Apple Watch, Polar or Suunto. Your trace appears instantly on the map.
steps.detail: Wondering if your format is compatible — yes, almost all GPS devices are supported.

steps.title: Customise your poster
steps.desc: Choose from dozens of map styles (satellite, terrain, minimal, watercolour...), adjust colours, typography, layout. Add your name, distance, time.
steps.detail: Every setting updates in real time — you see exactly what you'll download.

steps.title: Download your PDF
steps.desc: Secure payment €9 via Stripe. Instant high-resolution PDF download. Print at home or at a local printer — A3, A2 or A1 format.
steps.detail: The PDF is high-resolution (300 DPI) — perfect for large format printing.

features.label: Features
features.headline: Everything you need to create
features.subtitle: A professional editor, without the complexity. Everything you need to turn your GPX into a personalised race poster.

items.title: High-precision map
items.desc: GPS trace faithful to your route. Choose from 15+ map styles: satellite, terrain, minimal, vintage, night...

items.title: Varied layouts
items.desc: Portrait or landscape, full-bleed or with margins, trace only or with elevation profile. Dozens of combinations possible.

items.title: Pro typography
items.desc: 100+ Google Fonts available. Edit size, colour, weight, spacing. Everything you type updates in real time.

items.title: Elevation graph
items.desc: Display your altitude profile in style. Customise colours, graph height, and style (line or area).

items.title: Multi-activity
items.desc: Combine multiple runs on one poster. Perfect for stage challenges, training series, or running partners.

items.title: Ready-to-use templates
items.desc: Start with a stunning design and adapt it. Templates are created by designers — you start ahead.

gallery.label: Gallery
gallery.headline: Dozens of styles,
gallery.subtitle: From minimalist to colourful, vintage to high-tech — click a style to create it with your GPX.
gallery.cta: See all styles in the editor
gallery.ctaBottom: All these styles are free in the editor — PDF €9

urgency.label: Join the community
urgency.headline: These runners already have their poster.
urgency.subtitle: Every week, hundreds of runners turn their achievement into a poster. While you're hesitating, their marathon hangs proudly on the wall. Your next race won't wait.

skeptic.label: Not convinced yet?
skeptic.headline: Start by
skeptic.subtitle: You don't need to decide now. Import your GPX, play with the editor, see what your route looks like. Pay only if you're blown away.
skeptic.importGpxSub: No account, no card — see the result immediately

faqs.a: Yes. RunMemories supports GPX, KML, FIT — formats from Garmin, Apple Watch, Suunto, Polar, Wahoo. Or connect Strava directly in 1 click.
You download a high-resolution PDF (300 DPI) immediately after payment. Print at home, at a local printer, or online in A3, A2 or A1 format.

faqs.q: What if I don't like it?
faqs.a: Satisfaction guaranteed within 30 days, no questions asked. We refund in full if the result doesn't meet your expectations.
Preview is free and unlimited. Downloading the high-resolution PDF costs €9. One time, no subscription.

faqs.q: Can I give a RunMemories poster as a gift?
faqs.a: Yes, and it's probably the best gift for a runner. You can also create the poster from someone else's Strava activity.

footer.tagline: Turn your races into personalised posters. Import your GPX, customise, download your PDF. Your achievement deserves its frame.

costOfInaction.label: The cost of inaction
costOfInaction.headline: What does it cost you to
costOfInaction.subtitle: Every race that passes is a memory that fades. Blurry recollections. Forgotten stats. Emotions that dim.
costOfInaction.stat1Desc: 52 hours of your life to finish a marathon. You deserve better than a blurry photo as a memento.
costOfInaction.stat2Desc: Piled up with your Sunday selfies. Never looked at. Never framed. Never celebrated.
costOfInaction.stat3Label: price of a RunMemories PDF poster
costOfInaction.stat3Desc: To immortalise 52h of effort. That's €0.17 per hour of training invested.
costOfInaction.quote: Other runners already have their poster on the wall. What about you?
costOfInaction.quoteFootnote: While you read this, 12 runners are ordering their RunMemories poster.
